title: Static Analyzer Baselines

findings.name: Reentrancy
findings.explanation: External call appears before a later state write in the same function.

findings.name: Unprotected Ether Withdrawal
findings.explanation: Function transfers value or destroys funds without obvious owner/admin gating.

findings.name: Transaction Order Dependence
findings.explanation: Allowance is overwritten directly, which can expose approve race/front-running behavior.

findings.name: Unprotected Ether Withdrawal
findings.explanation: Function transfers value or destroys funds without obvious owner/admin gating.

findings.name: Unchecked Call Return Value
findings.explanation: Low-level call result is not clearly checked with require().

findings.explanation: Function transfers value or destroys funds without obvious owner/admin gating.

findings.name: Reentrancy
findings.explanation: Reentrancy in Token.withdraw(uint256) (data/generated_contracts/contract_001.sol#24-32): 	External calls: 	- (sent,None) = msg.sender.call{value: amount}() (data/generated_contracts/contract_001.sol#28) 	State variables written after the call(s): 	- balanceOf[msg.sender] -= amount (data/generated_contracts/contract_001.sol#31) 	Token.balanceOf (data/generated_contracts/contract_001.sol#7) can be used in cross function reentrancies: 	- Token.balanceOf (data/generated_contracts/contract_001.sol#7) 	- Token.constructor(string,uint256) (data/generated_contracts/contract_001.sol#10-14) 	- Token.transfer(address,uint256) (data/generated_contracts/contract_001.sol#16-21) 	- Token.withdraw(uint256) (data/generated_contracts/contract_001.sol#24-32)

findings.name: Integer Overflow/Underflow
findings.explanation: Version constraint ^0.8.0 contains known severe issues (https://solidity.readthedocs.io/en/latest/bugs.html) 	- FullInlinerNonExpressionSplitArgumentEvaluationOrder 	- MissingSideEffectsOnSelectorAccess 	- AbiReencodingHeadOverflowWithStaticArrayCleanup 	- DirtyBytesArrayToStorage 	- DataLocationChangeInInternalOverride 	- NestedCalldataArrayAbiReencodingSizeValidation 	- SignedImmutables 	- ABIDecodeTwoDimensionalArrayMemory 	- KeccakCaching. It is used by: 	- ^0.8.0 (data/generated_contracts/contract_001.sol#2)

findings.name: Unchecked Call Return Value
findings.explanation: Low level call in Token.withdraw(uint256) (data/generated_contracts/contract_001.sol#24-32): 	- (sent,None) = msg.sender.call{value: amount}() (data/generated_contracts/contract_001.sol#28)

findings.name: Integer Overflow/Underflow
findings.explanation: Version constraint ^0.8.0 contains known severe issues (https://solidity.readthedocs.io/en/latest/bugs.html) 	- FullInlinerNonExpressionSplitArgumentEvaluationOrder 	- MissingSideEffectsOnSelectorAccess 	- AbiReencodingHeadOverflowWithStaticArrayCleanup 	- DirtyBytesArrayToStorage 	- DataLocationChangeInInternalOverride 	- NestedCalldataArrayAbiReencodingSizeValidation 	- SignedImmutables 	- ABIDecodeTwoDimensionalArrayMemory 	- KeccakCaching. It is used by: 	- ^0.8.0 (data/generated_contracts/contract_002.sol#2)

findings.name: Unchecked Call Return Value
findings.explanation: Low level call in Vault.withdraw(uint256) (data/generated_contracts/contract_002.sol#18-23): 	- (success,None) = msg.sender.call{value: amount}() (data/generated_contracts/contract_002.sol#21)

findings.name: Unprotected Ether Withdrawal
findings.explanation: Auction.emergencyWithdraw() (data/generated_contracts/contract_004.sol#60-63) sends eth to arbitrary user 	Dangerous calls: 	- address(msg.sender).transfer(address(this).balance) (data/generated_contracts/contract_004.sol#62)

findings.name: Unprotected Ether Withdrawal
findings.explanation: Auction.withdraw() (data/generated_contracts/contract_004.sol#50-54) sends eth to arbitrary user 	Dangerous calls: 	- address(msg.sender).transfer(address(this).balance) (data/generated_contracts/contract_004.sol#53)

findings.name: Delegatecall to Untrusted Callee
findings.explanation: Token.upgrade(address,bytes) (data/generated_contracts/contract_006.sol#24-28) uses delegatecall to a input-controlled function id 	- (success,None) = newImplementation.delegatecall(data) (data/generated_contracts/contract_006.sol#26)

findings.name: Delegatecall to Untrusted Callee
findings.explanation: Token.upgrade(address,bytes).newImplementation (data/generated_contracts/contract_006.sol#24) lacks a zero-check on : 		- (success,None) = newImplementation.delegatecall(data) (data/generated_contracts/contract_006.sol#26)

findings.name: Unprotected Ether Withdrawal
findings.explanation: Consider introducing checks for `msg.sender` to ensure the recipient of the money is as intended.

findings.name: Reentrancy
findings.explanation: Changing state after an external call can lead to re-entrancy attacks.Use the checks-effects-interactions pattern to avoid this issue.

findings.name: Unprotected Ether Withdrawal
findings.explanation: Consider introducing checks for `msg.sender` to ensure the recipient of the money is as intended.

findings.name: Unprotected Ether Withdrawal
findings.explanation: Consider introducing checks for `msg.sender` to ensure the recipient of the money is as intended.

findings.name: Unchecked Call Return Value
findings.explanation: ERC20 functions may not behave as expected. For example: return values are not always meaningful. It is recommended to use OpenZeppelin's SafeERC20 library.

findings.name: Unprotected SELFDESTRUCT
findings.explanation: Remove the `selfdestruct` instruction from the code.

findings.name: Unprotected Ether Withdrawal
findings.explanation: It appears that the contract includes a payable function to accept Ether but lacks a corresponding function to withdraw it, which leads to the Ether being locked in the contract. To resolve this issue, please implement a public or external function that allows for the withdrawal of Ether from the contract.

findings.name: Delegatecall to Untrusted Callee
findings.explanation: Making a `delegatecall` to an arbitrary address without any checks is dangerous. Consider adding requirements on the target address.

findings.name: Unprotected Ether Withdrawal
findings.explanation: Consider introducing checks for `msg.sender` to ensure the recipient of the money is as intended.

findings.name: Unprotected Ether Withdrawal
findings.explanation: Consider introducing checks for `msg.sender` to ensure the recipient of the money is as intended.

findings.name: Reentrancy
findings.explanation: Changing state after an external call can lead to re-entrancy attacks.Use the checks-effects-interactions pattern to avoid this issue.

findings.name: Unchecked Call Return Value
findings.explanation: ERC20 functions may not behave as expected. For example: return values are not always meaningful. It is recommended to use OpenZeppelin's SafeERC20 library.

findings.name: Unprotected Ether Withdrawal
findings.explanation: Consider introducing checks for `msg.sender` to ensure the recipient of the money is as intended.

findings.name: Reentrancy
findings.explanation: Changing state after an external call can lead to re-entrancy attacks.Use the checks-effects-interactions pattern to avoid this issue.

findings.name: Unchecked Call Return Value
findings.explanation: The return value of the low-level call is not checked, so if the call fails, the Ether will be locked in the contract. If the low level is used to prevent blocking operations, consider logging failed calls. Ensure that the return value of a low-level call is checked or logged.

notes: The built-in heuristic comparator is deterministic and runs everywhere.
Slither and Aderyn adapters run real analyzer JSON output when their binaries are available on PATH.
Mythril remains listed as a planned symbolic-analysis comparator.
